title: Blackberry Jam

overview.description: A rich, intensely fruity blackberry jam with a bright acidity and soft, spreadable set.

method: Place a small plate in the freezer for testing the set later.
Place the blackberries and lemon juice in a large preserving pan.
Heat gently until the berries begin to release their juice.
Simmer for approximately 5 minutes until the fruit has softened.
Stir gently until the sugar has completely dissolved.
Bring the mixture to a rapid boil.
Begin testing the set by placing a small spoonful of jam on the chilled plate.
Leave it for a few seconds, then push the surface with your finger.
If the surface wrinkles slightly, the jam has reached setting point.
If it remains completely liquid, continue boiling and test again.
Remove any scum from the surface.
Pour the hot jam into hot sterilised jars.
Seal immediately and allow to cool.

learning.did-you-know: Blackberries contain natural pectin, but the amount varies depending on how ripe they are. Slightly underripe fruit generally contains more pectin than very ripe fruit.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How sugar, fruit acidity and pectin combine to create a set preserve and how to test jam for the correct setting point.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A simple homemade preserve that makes excellent use of fresh or frozen blackberries and provides a useful way to preserve seasonal fruit.
chapman-tips: A wide pan helps the jam boil efficiently and reduces evaporation time.
Don't boil the jam for too long; overcooking can produce a stiff, sticky texture.
Frozen blackberries can be used directly from frozen.
A few slightly underripe berries can help with setting.
The lemon juice brightens the flavour as well as helping with the setting process.
Blackberry seeds remain in the jam and contribute texture.
For a smoother jam, pass some or all of the cooked fruit through a sieve before adding the sugar.
The jam will thicken further as it cools.
notes.batch-cooking: Freezes well. Properly jarred jam is better suited to long-term storage.
notes.leftovers: Excellent in cakes, crumbles, sauces and desserts.
